description_long: This object is a realistic crab with a broad, oval-shaped carapace that is dark brown and mottled with lighter and darker patches. The dorsal side features two prominent claws (chelae) that are robust and slightly asymmetrical, with the right claw appearing slightly larger. The crab has eight segmented walking legs, each covered in a rough, textured exoskeleton with spiny protrusions and a gradient from brown to lighter tips. The ventral side is pale, almost white, with visible segmentation and jointed appendages. The eyes are positioned on short stalks at the front of the carapace, and the mouthparts are tucked beneath the front edge. The overall appearance suggests a hard, calcified shell typical of marine crabs, with a rugged, natural finish.
description: A detailed, lifelike crab with a mottled brown carapace, two large claws, eight spiny legs, and a pale segmented underside. The surface is rough and hard, with eyes on short stalks at the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad, dark carapace, both claws raised, and eyes on short stalks.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the body, the curvature of the claws, and the segmentation of the legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the pale, segmented underside and the arrangement of the legs and claws from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other profile of the crab, with visible leg segmentation and claw structure.
